quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Slope field"?

options.text: First derivative test: always works but requires sign analysis on an interval; second derivative test: simpler but can be inconclusive
Two functions are asymptotically equivalent if lim(x→∞) f(x)/g(x) = 1
A grid of short line segments showing the slope dy/dx = f(x,y) at each point; visual representation of a DE
F(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t)dt; represents the accumulated area from a to x; its derivative is f(x) by FTC Part 1

quiz_questions.explanation: "Slope field" is defined as: A grid of short line segments showing the slope dy/dx = f(x,y) at each point; visual representation of a DE. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Solution curve"?

options.text: A function can have more than one point satisfying MVT; each is where the tangent is parallel to the secant
A curve that is tangent to the slope field at every point; represents a particular solution of the DE
Use a constraint equation to express the objective function in terms of one variable before finding critical points
Derived from the limit definition using sin(x+h) = sin x cos h + cos x sin h and special limits

quiz_questions.explanation: "Solution curve" is defined as: A curve that is tangent to the slope field at every point; represents a particular solution of the DE. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Equilibrium solution"?

options.text: The set of input values that make physical sense in the context; endpoints may produce absolute extrema
The + C in every indefinite integral accounts for the fact that F(x) + C has the same derivative for any constant C
A constant solution y = c where f(x,c) = 0 for all x; appears as horizontal segments in the slope field
F(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t)dt; represents the accumulated area from a to x; its derivative is f(x) by FTC Part 1

quiz_questions.explanation: "Equilibrium solution" is defined as: A constant solution y = c where f(x,c) = 0 for all x; appears as horizontal segments in the slope field. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Euler's method"?

options.text: Divide the plate into thin horizontal strips of width dh; each strip has approximately constant pressure ρgh and area w(h)·dh
[f(b)-f(a)]/(b-a); the average rate of change over [a,b]; the slope of the line connecting endpoints
A function can have more than one point satisfying MVT; each is where the tangent is parallel to the secant
Numerical approximation: start at (x₀,y₀), step by yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + f(xₙ,yₙ)·Δx with step size Δx

quiz_questions.explanation: "Euler's method" is defined as: Numerical approximation: start at (x₀,y₀), step by yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + f(xₙ,yₙ)·Δx with step size Δx. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Euler's method accuracy"?

options.text: P = K: if the population is perturbed slightly above or below K, it returns to K
State the theorem precisely, verify hypotheses, apply the conclusion, and interpret in the context of the problem
Smaller step size Δx gives better accuracy but requires more steps; error is approximately proportional to Δx
Two functions are asymptotically equivalent if lim(x→∞) f(x)/g(x) = 1

quiz_questions.explanation: "Euler's method accuracy" is defined as: Smaller step size Δx gives better accuracy but requires more steps; error is approximately proportional to Δx. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A grid of short line segments showing the slope dy/dx = f(x,y) at each point; visual representation of a DE"?

options.text: Position, velocity, acceleration
Second derivative
Slope field
Definite integral

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Slope field".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A curve that is tangent to the slope field at every point; represents a particular solution of the DE"?

options.text: Solution curve
d/dx[sin x] = cos x
Definite integral
Newton's method

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Solution curve".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A constant solution y = c where f(x,c) = 0 for all x; appears as horizontal segments in the slope field"?

options.text: Equilibrium solution
Derivative of tan x
Integration by parts formula
d/dx[cos x] = -sin x

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Equilibrium solution".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Numerical approximation: start at (x₀,y₀), step by yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + f(xₙ,yₙ)·Δx with step size Δx"?

options.text: Lorenz curve
Units of derivative
Zero derivative implies constant
Euler's method

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Euler's method".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Smaller step size Δx gives better accuracy but requires more steps; error is approximately proportional to Δx"?

options.text: Euler's method accuracy
Limit does not exist
Exponential function
MVT with restrictions

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Euler's method accuracy".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Slope Fields and Solutions is accurate?

options.text: Slope Fields and Solutions visualize differential equations geometrically.
Basic Derivative Rules provide shortcuts for finding derivatives without using the limit definition every time.
Area Between Curves computes the area enclosed between two functions.
When k > 0: exponential growth (population, investment); when k < 0: exponential decay (radioactive decay, drug clearance).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Slope Fields and Solutions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: When k > 0: exponential growth (population, investment); when k < 0: exponential decay (radioactive decay, drug clearance).
Phase line analysis reveals stability: P = 0 is unstable (arrows point away), P = K is stable (arrows point toward).
A slope field (direction field) displays short line segments at grid points (x,y), each with slope equal to f(x,y) from dy/dx = f(x,y).

The method: (1) identify the quantity to optimize, (2) express it as a function of one variable using constraints, (3) find critical points where f'(x) = 0, (4) verify maxima/minima using the first or second derivative test, (5) check endpoints...

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Slope Fields and Solutions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: Understanding growth rates is essential for comparing algorithms, modeling populations, and analyzing asymptotic behavior.
The sum, difference, product, quotient, and power laws allow limits to be computed piece by piece.
Solution curves follow the direction of these segments.
Solutions are functions y(x) that satisfy the equation.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Slope Fields and Solutions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: Slope fields reveal qualitative behavior without solving: equilibrium solutions (horizontal segments), stability, and convergence/divergence of solutions.
Phase line analysis reveals stability: P = 0 is unstable (arrows point away), P = K is stable (arrows point toward).
For logarithms: d/dx[ln x] = 1/x and d/dx[logₐ x] = 1/(x ln a).
Success requires clear justification of setup, correct antiderivatives, appropriate use of technology, and precise interpretation of results in the given context.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Slope Fields and Solutions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: The method: (1) identify the quantity to optimize, (2) express it as a function of one variable using constraints, (3) find critical points where f'(x) = 0, (4) verify maxima/minima using the first or second derivative test, (5) check endpoints...
If f'(x) > 0 on an interval, then f is increasing on that interval; if f'(x) < 0, f is decreasing.
Euler's method provides numerical approximation: yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + f(xₙ,yₙ)·Δx.
Arc Length and Surface Area uses integration to measure curve length and rotational surface area.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Slope Fields and Solutions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: Population dynamics: given a birth rate minus death rate function, calculate total population change over a decade
Medical research: sketching dose-response curves to identify therapeutic windows (effective dose ranges) for drug development
Error analysis in numerical methods: understanding limits helps predict how rounding errors accumulate as step sizes approach zero in computer calculations
Weather prediction: numerical methods similar to Euler's method are used to solve atmospheric differential equations on supercomputers for weather forecasting

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Slope Fields and Solutions.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: Trajectory simulation in video games and animations: Euler's method updates position and velocity at each frame using the governing differential equations
Submarine hull design: calculating pressure at operating depth determines hull thickness needed to prevent collapse
Velocity as the derivative of position: v(t) = s'(t) tells you the instantaneous speed and direction of a moving object at any moment
Finding the slope of a circle x² + y² = 25 at any point: dy/dx = -x/y, giving the tangent line direction for navigation and engineering applications

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Slope Fields and Solutions.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Slope Fields and Solutions applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Average temperature: f_avg = (1/24)∫₀²⁴ T(t)dt gives the average temperature over a 24-hour period, used for energy consumption estimates
Long-term drug concentration models: horizontal asymptotes represent the steady-state drug level that the body approaches with regular dosing
Radioactive decay: integrating the decay rate dN/dt = -λN gives N(t) = N₀e^(-λt), the fundamental decay equation
Population forecasting: slope fields of population DEs visually show whether populations are growing, shrinking, or approaching equilibrium

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Slope Fields and Solutions.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Slope Fields and Solutions?

options.text: Water tank problems: given inflow rate and outflow rate functions, compute water level at any time t and find when the tank is fullest
Compound interest with variable rates: dA/dt for A = P·e^(∫r(t)dt) requires the chain rule combined with the FTC for variable-rate financial modeling
Disease spread: the SIR model's infection curve resembles logistic growth, with the inflection point marking peak new infections
Weather prediction: numerical methods similar to Euler's method are used to solve atmospheric differential equations on supercomputers for weather forecasting

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Slope Fields and Solutions.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Slope Fields and Solutions in practice?

options.text: Planetary orbits: elliptical orbits x²/a² + y²/b² = 1 analyzed with implicit differentiation to find orbital velocity direction at each point
Moore's Law: the roughly exponential growth of transistor density in microchips (doubling every ~2 years) eventually creating unforeseen limits in physics
Trajectory simulation in video games and animations: Euler's method updates position and velocity at each frame using the governing differential equations
Understanding S-curves in product adoption: inflection point marks the transition from rapid early adoption growth to market saturation deceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Slope Fields and Solutions.
